diary: Grey drizzle followed me right out of bed this morning in Bothell. Before I even headed out, I checked the rooftop herbs—still damp, still stubborn—then watched my breath fog the kitchen window like it was trying to talk me into staying home. The drive toward Issaquah felt extra careful with the rain, and by the time I rolled past SR-520’s bridge chaos (eastbound shut 9am to noon), traffic was doing that tense shuffle thing. I got into the studio and just sank into the tracking/editing room, calibrating levels and cleaning up vocal takes until my ears felt properly “settled.” At QFC I grabbed a warm lunch and caught up on messages, then did a careful playback pass for the deliverables. After work, Safeway for dinner stuff, long way home through the detours, then a cozy cook and indoor strength at LA Fitness. Tonight at Northshore Food Bank, we kept the lines moving despite the damp world. I feel… steadier than the weather.
thought_process: I tried to choose the day’s rhythm around what the city threw at me. The SR-520 closure made me plan for extra commute time, so I left with caution and protected the studio blocks—tracking and editing are the kind of work that doesn’t forgive rushing. In the morning, I leaned into calibration and vocal cleanup because the rain made everything feel slower anyway; focusing on details helped me regain control. Lunch was simple and nearby so I could check messages without losing momentum. On the way back, I intentionally took the longer route to avoid stress spillover. The grocery run felt practical, not indulgent—warm food matched the day. Volunteering afterward at the Northshore Food Bank shifted my mood; even in a drizzly, capped-mobility day, I could still help things move.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
